FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class ReorderList

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

Reorder geometry for a one-axis item strip (the WinUI live-reorder model — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp): given the items' resting main-axis extents and the dragged item's accumulated drag delta (DragEventArgs.TotalDy for a vertical list / TotalDx for a horizontal strip), it computes

  • the PENDING insertion slot under the pointer (midpoint rule: the dragged item's centre crossing a sibling's midpoint claims its slot — the engine analogue of IItemLookupPanel.GetClosestElementInfo + LiveReorderHelper::MovedItems::GetDragOverIndex, ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:984-1063),
  • the dwell-committed TARGET slot — WinUI moves displaced items only after the drag-over index has been stable for the live-reorder timer (200ms list / 300ms grid — LISTVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER / GRIDVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER, ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:50-51), and
  • per-sibling displacement hints (OffsetFor(int)): items between the dragged slot and the target shift by one full dragged-extent (+spacing), the live-reorder "items part to make room" motion (LiveReorderTimerTickHandler → MoveItemsForLiveReorder, ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:2125-2158).

Consumers (ListView/GridView/TabView/TreeView item hosts) wire it to the drag lifecycle: Begin in OnDragStarted, Update(e.TotalDy) in OnDragDelta, Advance(dtMs) from the frame clock; when Advance commits a new target, re-render with the children ORDER projected to it (ProjectOrder(Span<int>) — stable keys keep node identity) and a LayoutTransition (Animate) on every item: displaced siblings genuinely change slots, so the engine's FLIP pipeline animates the part-to-make-room motion, and Input.DragController re-anchors the pointer-held visual to its moved slot (no jump). OffsetFor(int) remains for offset-hint consumers — but an authored OffsetX/Y hint must NOT be combined with Animate on the same node: FLIP position tracks own the whole translate channel (CompositeOp.Replace — AnimEngine.ReframePosition) and would stomp the hint at seed and settle. Complete() in OnDragCompleted fires OnCommit with the collection move; Cancel() in OnDragCanceled drops every hint. All state is grow-only — steady-state reorder (drag at pointer rate) allocates nothing.

TWO-DIMENSIONAL MODE (Begin2D(int, int, int) / Update2D(float, float, float, float) / OffsetFor2D(int, float, float, out float, out float)) absorbs the GridView live-reorder geometry: the pending slot comes from the dragged tile's accumulated (dx,dy) against the grid (row from the vertical stride, column from the realized column width), with the 300ms grid dwell (GRIDVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER = 300ms — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:51) re-armed on every drag-over change. A one-slot grid shift can WRAP A ROW (the trailing tile of a row dropping to the head of the next), so the 2-D displacement hint carries BOTH axes. The dwell ticker (Advance(float)), the moved-items projection (ProjectOrder(Span<int>) — row-major, identical to 1-D), Complete() and Cancel() are shared with the 1-D path unchanged. Columns = 0 selects the 1-D path; Begin2D(int, int, int) sets it.

public sealed class ReorderList

Fields

GridDwellMs

GridView's dwell — GRIDVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER = 300ms (ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:51).

public const float GridDwellMs = 300

Field Value

float

ListDwellMs

Hint dwell before displaced items shift — LISTVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER = 200ms (ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:50).

public const float ListDwellMs = 200

Field Value

float

OnCommit

The collection move, fired by Complete() when the item lands on a new slot: (fromIndex, toIndex) in the ORIGINAL order — remove at from, insert at to.

public Action<int, int>? OnCommit

Field Value

Action<int, int>

Properties

Columns

Grid column count for the 2-D mode (0 ⇒ 1-D mode). Set by Begin2D(int, int, int); the 1-D Begin(int, ReadOnlySpan<float>, float) overloads leave it 0.

public int Columns { get; }

Property Value

int

Count 

public int Count { get; }

Property Value

int

DraggedIndex 

public int DraggedIndex { get; }

Property Value

int

DraggedTargetStart

The resting main-axis start the dragged item will occupy at the current TargetIndex — the settle target for a consumer-drawn drop preview.

public float DraggedTargetStart { get; }

Property Value

float

DwellMs

Dwell before a new pending slot becomes the shown target (WinUI restarts the timer on every drag-over index change — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:1068-1074). 0 ⇒ hints follow the pointer immediately.

public float DwellMs { get; set; }

Property Value

float

IsActive 

public bool IsActive { get; }

Property Value

bool

PendingIndex

The latest computed insertion slot under the pointer (becomes TargetIndex after the dwell).

public int PendingIndex { get; }

Property Value

int

TargetIndex

The dwell-committed slot the displacement hints currently show (= DraggedIndex until the first dwell elapses).

public int TargetIndex { get; }

Property Value

int

Methods

Advance(float)

Advance the live-reorder dwell (drive from the frame clock while a drag is active). Returns true when the shown target changed — re-render the siblings with the new OffsetFor(int) hints (their LayoutTransition FLIP animates the shift).

public bool Advance(float dtMs)

Parameters

dtMs float

Returns

bool

Begin(int, int, float, float)

Uniform-extent overload (fixed-row ListView / tab strip): all count items share itemExtent.

public void Begin(int draggedIndex, int count, float itemExtent, float spacing = 0)

Parameters

draggedIndex int
count int
itemExtent float
spacing float

Begin(int, ReadOnlySpan<float>, float)

Start a reorder for draggedIndex over items with the given resting main-axis itemExtents, separated by spacing (the container's Gap). Copies the extents into grow-only storage (no per-drag steady alloc once grown).

public void Begin(int draggedIndex, ReadOnlySpan<float> itemExtents, float spacing = 0)

Parameters

draggedIndex int
itemExtents ReadOnlySpan<float>
spacing float

Begin2D(int, int, int)

Begin a 2-D (grid) reorder for draggedIndex over count tiles laid out row-major in columns columns (absorbs the GridView live-reorder geometry). No resting extents are stored — the 2-D slot math is grid-geometric (Update2D(float, float, float, float) takes the realized column width and row stride per move). Sets Columns > 0 to select the 2-D path.

public void Begin2D(int draggedIndex, int count, int columns)

Parameters

draggedIndex int
count int
columns int

Cancel()

Abort the reorder (drag canceled): drop every hint without committing.

public void Cancel()

Complete()

Finish the reorder at the LATEST pending slot (the release point under the pointer — the dwell shown state never delays the actual drop). Resets all hints BEFORE firing OnCommit, so the commit's re-render reads zero offsets. Returns the destination index (−1 when idle).

public int Complete()

Returns

int

MoveTarget(int)

Keyboard lift-mode move (rbd a11y — E5-L3): shift the shown target directly by delta slots, clamped to the list, with NO dwell (a deliberate keystroke needs no stabilization timer; pending and target stay in lockstep so Complete() commits exactly what the user sees). Returns true when the shown target changed — re-render with ProjectOrder(Span<int>) and the FLIP pipeline animates the move.

public bool MoveTarget(int delta)

Parameters

delta int

Returns

bool

Move<T>(IList<T>, int, int)

Apply a committed reorder to a collection in place: remove at from, insert at to — exactly the OnCommit payload, and exactly WinUI's drop commit (ListViewBase::ReorderItemsTo → RemoveAt(realItemIndex) + InsertAt(insertIndex), ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:1536-1537). Out-of-range / no-op indices are ignored. Cold path (one drop).

public static void Move<T>(IList<T> list, int from, int to)

Parameters

list IList<T>
from int
to int

Type Parameters

T

OffsetFor(int)

The main-axis displacement hint for sibling index at the current shown target: items between the dragged slot and the target shift one dragged-extent (+spacing) to make room; everything else (and the dragged item itself) is 0.

public float OffsetFor(int index)

Parameters

index int

Returns

float

OffsetFor2D(int, float, float, out float, out float)

The 2-D per-tile displacement hint at the current shown target: a tile in the block between the dragged slot and the target shifts by ONE slot toward the vacated source (row-major), which can WRAP A ROW — so the hint carries both axes (dx, dy). The dragged tile and everything outside the block get (0,0). A verbatim port of the GridView 2-D OffsetFor (ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:2125-2158).

public void OffsetFor2D(int index, float colWidth, float rowStride, out float dx, out float dy)

Parameters

index int
colWidth float
rowStride float
dx float
dy float

ProjectOrder(Span<int>)

Fill order (length ≥ Count) with ORIGINAL item indices in the current dwell-committed projected order: the dragged item occupies TargetIndex, everything else keeps its relative order (the live-reorder "moved items" view — LiveReorderHelper::MovedItems, ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:2125-2157). Re-render the children from this projection (stable keys!) with a LayoutTransition and the FLIP pipeline animates the displaced siblings. Span-filling — 0 alloc.

public void ProjectOrder(Span<int> order)

Parameters

order Span<int>

Update(float)

Recompute the pending slot from the dragged item's accumulated main-axis translation (DragEventArgs.TotalDy / TotalDx). Midpoints are evaluated in RESTING coordinates, so the hint motion never feeds back into the slot math. Returns true when the pending slot changed (the dwell re-arms — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:1068-1074).

public bool Update(float dragDelta)

Parameters

dragDelta float

Returns

bool

Update2D(float, float, float, float)

Recompute the 2-D pending slot from the dragged tile's accumulated translation (totalDx, totalDy): column from colWidth, row from rowStride, clamped to the grid. Returns true when the pending slot changed (the dwell re-arms to DwellMs — already 300 for the grid preset; ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:1068-1074). A verbatim port of the GridView 2-D update.

public bool Update2D(float totalDx, float totalDy, float colWidth, float rowStride)

Parameters

totalDx float
totalDy float
colWidth float
rowStride float

Returns

bool
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
